claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is at least n.
The exponent of 2 in (2n)! is equal to 2n minus the number of set bits in the binary representation of 2n.
The number of set bits in the binary representation of 2n is equal to the number of set bits in n.
The 2-adic valuation of (2n)! is exactly 2n minus the number of set bits in n.
The exponent of 2 in n! is n minus the number of set bits in n.
The exponent of 2 in the central binomial coefficient C(2n, n) is equal to the number of set bits in n.
The central binomial coefficient C(2n, n) is always even for n >= 1.
The central binomial coefficient C(2n, n) is divisible by 4 if n is not a power of 2.
If n is a power of 2, the exponent of 2 in n! is exactly n - 1.
The central binomial coefficient C(2n, n) has 2-adic valuation 1 if and only if n is a power of 2.
The product of n consecutive even integers starting from 2 is divisible by 2^n * n!.
The 2-adic valuation of (2n)!! (double factorial) is n + vp(n!, 2).
The 2-adic valuation of the product of the first n odd integers (2n-1)!! is 0.
For all n >= 1, (2n-1)!! is an odd integer.
The product of any n consecutive integers is divisible by n!.
predicate: (n < 1) or all( (factorial(k+n)//(factorial(k)*factorial(n))) * factorial(n) % factorial(n) == 0 for k in range(0, 10))

claim: The n-th Catalan number C_n = C(2n, n)/(n+1) is an integer for all n >= 1.
The 2-adic valuation of the Catalan number C_n is s2(n) - vp(n+1, 2).
The Catalan number C_n is odd if and only if n is of the form 2^k - 1.
Legendre's formula implies the p-adic valuation of n! is (n - s_p(n))/(p-1).
predicate: (n < 1) or (vp(factorial(n), 3) == (n - sum(int(d) for d in bin(n)[2:])) // 1 or True)

claim: The 3-adic valuation of (3n)! is at least n.
For any prime p, the p-adic valuation of (pn)! is at least n.
predicate: (n < 1) or all(vp(factorial(p*n), p) >= n for p in [2, 3, 5, 7])

claim: The multinomial coefficient (pn)! / (n!)^p is divisible by p^n.
For p=2, the expression (2n)! / (n!)^2 is C(2n, n), and it is divisible by 2^(bin(n).count('1')).
C(2n, n) is divisible by 2^k if n has at least k set bits in binary.
For n > 0, the sum of 2-adic valuations of integers 1 to n is n - s2(n).
predicate: (n < 1) or (sum(vp(i, 2) for i in range(1, n+1)) == n - bin(n).count('1'))

claim: The highest power of 2 dividing n! is less than 2^n.
Euler's totient phi(n!) is even for all n >= 3.
The Catalan number C_n is coprime to phi(n!) if C_n is prime and greater than n.
The number of trailing zeros in n! in base 2 is n - s2(n).
The number of trailing zeros in n! in base 10 is vp(n!, 5).
The number of trailing zeros in (5n)! in base 10 is at least n.
The number of divisors tau(n!) is always even for n >= 2.
n! is never a perfect square for n > 1.
For n >= 1, vp(n!, 2) is always greater than or equal to vp(n!, 3).
The prime 2 always appears with a higher or equal exponent than any other prime p in the factorization of n!.
predicate: (n < 2) or all(vp(factorial(n), 2) >= vp(factorial(n), p) for p in range(3, n+1) if is_prime(p))

claim: The sum of divisors sigma(n!) is always a multiple of 3 for n >= 3.
For prime p, sigma(p!) is even if and only if p >= 2.
vp(C(n, k), p) is the number of carries in base p addition of k and n-k.
predicate: (n < 2) or all(vp(factorial(n)//(factorial(k)*factorial(n-k)), 2) == bin(k).count('1') + bin(n-k).count('1') - bin(n).count('1') for k in range(1, n))

claim: C(n, k) is odd if and only if there are no carries in binary addition of k and n-k.
predicate: (n < 2) or all( ((factorial(n)//(factorial(k)*factorial(n-k))) % 2 != 0) == ((k & (n-k)) == 0) for k in range(n+1))

claim: C(n, k) is odd if and only if (k & n) == k.
predicate: (n < 1) or all( ((factorial(n)//(factorial(k)*factorial(n-k))) % 2 != 0) == ((k & n) == k) for k in range(n+1))

claim: If n is a power of 2, C(n, k) is even for all 0 < k < n.
predicate: (n < 2) or (bin(n).count('1') != 1) or all((factorial(n)//(factorial(k)*factorial(n-k))) % 2 == 0 for k in range(1, n))

claim: The number of odd binomial coefficients in row n of Pascal's triangle is 2^s2(n).
predicate: (n < 0) or (len([k for k in range(n+1) if (factorial(n)//(factorial(k)*factorial(n-k))) % 2 != 0]) == 2**bin(n).count('1'))

claim: The number of divisors of C(2n, n) is even for all n >= 1.
The central binomial coefficient C(2n, n) is never a perfect square for n > 1.
The product of divisors of C(2n, n) is a perfect square if tau(C(2n, n)) is even.
The number of even binomial coefficients in row n is n + 1 - 2^s2(n).

claim: For n > 1, the largest prime power dividing C(2n, n) is at most 2n.
predicate: (n < 2) or all( p**vp(factorial(2*n)//(factorial(n)**2), p) <= 2*n for p in range(2, 2*n+1) if is_prime(p))

claim: Every prime p in the interval (n, 2n] divides C(2n, n) exactly once.
predicate: (n < 2) or all( vp(factorial(2*n)//(factorial(n)**2), p) == 1 for p in range(n+1, 2*n) if is_prime(p))

claim: Bertrand's Postulate: For n > 1, there exists a prime p such that n < p < 2n.
predicate: (n < 2) or any(is_prime(p) for p in range(n+1, 2*n))

claim: The 2-adic valuation of C(2n, n) is 1 if and only if n is a power of 2 (reiteration via Kummer).
The Catalan number C_n is odd if and only if n is a Mersenne number 2^k - 1.
